risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

sect1.title: Chapter 18
sect1.para: 1. c. All additional medical or surgical services required of the surgeon during the postoperative period of the surgery to address complications that do not require additional trips to the OR are included in the global package (not separately reported) under Medicare policy.

2. b. Stabilization (eg, temporary cast/splint/strap) that is performed to protect an injury and provide comfort to the patient until another physician performs initial fracture or dislocation care is reported with the appropriate E/M and casting/splinting/strapping codes.
3. b. False. There is noage limit for cardiac catheterization for congenital heart defect (93593–93598). If there is evaluation of congenital cardiac defects, it is appropriate to report these codes.

4. c. When an existing central venous access device is removed and a new one placed via a separate venous access site, appropriate codes for both procedures (removal of old, if code exists, and insertion of new device) should be reported. Refer to replacement codes for removal with replacement via the same venous access site.

5. d. Excision or repair of strangulated organs or structures (eg, 44120, enterectomy, resection of small intestine; single resection and anastomosis) is reported in addition to the repair of a strangulated hernia.
